lyrics:
Climb aboard with a heart wide open
Pack your dreams, keep hope your destination
Rolling past the days we missed
Waving thanks with every twist

On the Gratitude Express
No ticket, just a yes
Every mile is a blessing
From the start until the ending
Got no baggage, just this song
Grateful to be riding from now on

Feelin’ fine on the Gratitude Express
No regrets on the Gratitude Express

Met a child with eyes like summer
Taught me joy like no other
Elder smiled with wrinkled grace
Said “Every stop leaves a trace”

On the Gratitude Express
No ticket, just a yes
Every mile is a blessing
From the start until the ending
Got no baggage, just this song
Grateful to be riding from now on

Feelin’ fine on the Gratitude Express
No regrets on the Gratitude Express

So many moments, small and bright
Like lanterns in the night
They light the tracks behind, ahead
With every word I’ve ever said

On the Gratitude Express
No ticket, just a yes
Every mile is a blessing
From the start until the ending
Got no baggage, just this song
Grateful to be riding from now on

Feelin’ fine on the Gratitude Express
No regrets on the Gratitude Express
Sunshine ride on the Gratitude Express
Come on board the Gratitude Express…
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track unfolds with a hopeful and anthemic atmosphere, building from clean, arpeggiated guitars into a full-band, driving rock texture. The energy is consistently high and propulsive, creating a forward-moving, motivational feel that is both earnest and powerful. The production is polished and spacious, with a tight rhythm section anchoring wide guitars and a clear lead vocal that sits confidently atop the mix without feeling crowded. The most distinctive quality is the powerful lift into the expansive, sing-along chorus, which serves as the track's emotional and energetic peak, perfectly delivering its positive message.

review.review_text: Lingua Aeternum stepping into straight-ahead anthemic rock is a genuine surprise, and 'Gratitude Express' earns it: a waltz-time ride at a brisk 152 BPM in D major, where arpeggiated guitar opens the track before the full band kicks in and the chorus simply lifts. 'No ticket, just a yes' lands as a hook because it's earned by the verse's setup, not just stated, and the sing-along 'Feelin' fine on the Gratitude Express' payoff has the same uncomplicated momentum you'd find in early Tom Petty at his most road-ready. The mood tags suggest melancholy, but this is mostly sunny side up, the 'small and bright / like lanterns in the night' bridge being the one moment where weight quietly acknowledges itself before the final chorus sweeps it forward.
